items.label: storefront-dev
messages: Good, I've read the full spec. Now let me explore the existing codebase structure.
The component files already exist. Let me check the test files and see if they need work.
All files already exist. Let me run the tests to verify they pass.
All 26 tests pass. Now let me validate the dev server works with the overrides.
Dev server returned HTTP 200 — all modules resolve correctly. Let me now check the validation hook and ensure there are no SSR safety issues.

Everything checks out. The feature is already fully implemented:  1. ✅ **ProductTile override** (`overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx`) - wraps base tile with Quick View overlay bar 2. ✅ **QuickViewModal component** (`overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx`) - modal with ProductView, loading spinner, error state 3. ✅ **ProductTile tests** (`overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js`) - 12 tests covering rendering, interaction, accessibility 4. ✅ **QuickViewModal tests** (`overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js`) - 14 tests covering modal shell, content, accessibility 5. ✅ **All 26 unit tests pass** 6. ✅ **Dev server returns HTTP 200** - webpack resolves all modules 7. ✅ **SSR safety** - no window/document access, no Date.now/Math.random 8. ✅ **No networkidle in e2e tests** 9. ✅ **validate-app.sh** exists with baseline checks  Now let me commit and mark the pipeline complete.

The `storefront-dev` phase for `product-quick-view` is complete. Here's a summary:  ## Deliverables Verified  | File | Status | |---|---| | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx` | ✅ ProductTile override with Quick View overlay bar | | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js` | ✅ 12 unit tests passing | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx` | ✅ QuickViewModal with spinner/error/ProductView states | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js` | ✅ 14 unit tests passing |  ## Key Implementation Details  - **Overlay bar**: Full-width semi-transparent dark bar at bottom of product image, slides up on hover (desktop), always visible on mobile - **QuickViewModal**: Reuses `useProductViewModal` hook and `ProductView` component from base template - **Error handling**: Shows "product no longer available" state when fetch returns null - **Accessibility**: `aria-label` with product name, keyboard focusable, Escape closes modal - **Corner cases**: Sets/bundles excluded, missing productId guarded, `preventDefault`/`stopPropagation` on bar click - **100% line coverage** on both components - **Dev server HTTP 200** confirmed — all webpack modules resolve correctly

shellCommands.command: ls -la /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/components/ 2>/dev/null || echo "No components overrides dir yet"; ls -la /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/ 2>

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && npm start & ↵ SERVER_PID=$! ↵ echo "Server PID: $SERVER_PID" ↵ # Wait for webpack compilation ↵ for i in $(seq 1 90); do ↵ sleep 2 ↵ HTTP_STATUS=$(c

# SSR safety checklist ↵ echo "=== Checking for window/document access outside guards ===" ↵ grep -n 'window\|document\.' /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/components/product
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh all "feat(storefront): add product quick-view overlay bar and modal"
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view storefront-dev
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark storefront-dev complete for product-quick-view"

items.label: storefront-unit-test

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && find overrides -name "*.test.*" 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; find overrides -name "__tests__" -type d 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; cat package.json | grep -

messages: Good, I can see the components were created. Let me read them and understand the test patterns.
Good - the dev agent already created test files. Let me run them to see if they pass.
All 26 tests pass. Let me check the full output to see if the product-tile tests also passed and review coverage.

All 26 tests pass across both test suites with 100% statement, function, and line coverage. The tests cover:  **QuickViewModal (14 tests):** Modal shell (spinner, product view, test IDs, aria-label, open/close), content integration (product data, full details link, add-to-cart button, prop forwarding), and accessibility (Escape key, aria-label fallback).  **ProductTile (12 tests):** Overlay bar rendering (standard product, icon+text, aria-label, sets/bundles excluded, missing productId), interactions (click opens modal, preventDefault, stopPropagation, close), and visual states (role="group").  Now let me also verify the rest of the test suite isn't broken.
There's 1 failed test suite. Let me see the full output to find which one.
